articles.title: An explosion at a fireworks plant in China kills at least 26 people, state media says
articles.summary: Authorities said search and rescue at a fireworks plant in a central Chinese province has largely been completed, but verification of the casualties and identification of the victims are still underwa

articles.title: As Oil Prices Stay High, China Doubles Down on Wind Power
articles.summary: An industrial policy of subsidies and import restrictions laid the foundations for China to become almost as dominant in wind turbines as in solar panels.

articles.title: Ships Under Fire in Strait of Hormuz, and Kennedy’s Pushback on Antidepressants
articles.summary: Plus, the gowns — and the backlash — at the Met Gala.

articles.title: This flashy group of Portland mall-walkers puts neon pep into step
articles.summary: Krista Catwood wanted a fun way to exercise, so she started a mall-walking group whose unofficial costume was 1980s garb. The festive, all-ages event has exploded in popularity over the past year.

articles.title: The Growing Rift Between the UAE and Saudi Arabia, Explained
articles.summary: Officials say competition between the countries is healthy. But tensions are mounting over energy quotas, regional conflicts and their different visions for the Middle East.

articles.title: Minneapolis immigrants still feeling the sting of Trump's largest crackdown yet
articles.summary: The impact of Operation Metro Surge in the Twin Cities can still be felt, even three months after it ended.  For immigrants, the impact has been devastating — many are at risk of losing their homes an

articles.title: In Venezuela, Trump Vowed to Show Accountability. But Secret Oil Deals Linger.
articles.summary: Trump officials and their Venezuelan allies have promised a new era of accountability to unlock Venezuela’s immense oil wealth. But the country’s oil industry remains a black hole.

articles.title: Primary elections in Ohio, Indiana offer latest temperature check on Trump popularity
articles.summary: The president seeks to oust Indiana Republicans who blocked a redistricting push in that state, while growing dissatisfaction with Trump's agenda gives Democrats an opening in Ohio.

articles.title: Cruise ship waiting for help after 3 people died in a suspected hantavirus outbreak
articles.summary: A cruise ship with nearly 150 people aboard was waiting for help off the coast of Cape Verde in the Atlantic Ocean after three passengers died in a suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus.

articles.title: Russia declares a truce in Ukraine to mark Victory Day
articles.summary: Russia declared a unilateral ceasefire in Ukraine for Friday and Saturday to mark the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II, but threatened to strike back at Kyiv if it tries to disrupt festivities.

articles.title: U.S. Military Strikes Boat in Caribbean, Killing 2
articles.summary: The aerial strike raised the death toll to at least 187 in the U.S. campaign against boats the Trump administration accuses of smuggling drugs.

articles.title: Wyoming lawmakers use pro-natalist arguments to justify proposed new partial abortion ban
articles.summary: Wyoming lawmakers say tight new restrictions on abortion are needed to grow the state's population. Demographers and others say economic conditions are more to blame for an exodus of young people.

articles.title: Trump Administration Demands Names of 2020 Election Workers in Georgia
articles.summary: The Justice Department again appears to be using the investigative power of the federal government to rehash debunked claims that Democrats stole the 2020 election.

articles.title: Trump Tries to Downplay Economic Effects of the Iran War
articles.summary: At a White House event for Small Business Week, he described the economy as “roaring” and predicted that gas prices would go down soon.

articles.title: See the looks from the 2026 Met Gala red carpet
articles.summary: Extravagantly dressed celebrities and designers made their way up the famous Met staircase Monday evening. The fundraiser is one of the fashion industry's biggest nights.

articles.title: Soil at D.C. Golf Course Where East Wing Debris Was Dumped Contains Toxic Metals
articles.summary: The National Park Service said the dump of debris, between the fourth and ninth holes, does not exceed environmental limits. Opponents disagree.

articles.title: Why U.S. troops are stationed in Germany, and what happens if some leave
articles.summary: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with Jeff Rathke, president of the American-German Institute, about U.S. troops stationed in Germany, and what happens if President Trump moves some of them elsewhere.

articles.title: Modi's party takes control of India's West Bengal in key state election
articles.summary: The outcome is expected to strengthen Modi's political position midway through his third term.

articles.title: U.S. tries to force open the Strait of Hormuz as the UAE comes under attack
articles.summary: The U.S. military said it fired on Iranian forces and sank six small boats as it moved to reopen the Strait of Hormuz on Monday. The United Arab Emirates said it had come under attack from Iran.

articles.title: Trump says U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships through the Strait. And, key Senate races to watch
articles.summary: President Trump announced that the U.S will "guide" stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz. And, Democrats face an uphill battle to gain Senate control. Here are the key races to watch.

articles.title: Rescuers help Timmy, a stranded whale, return home
articles.summary: Rescuers defy warnings as the stranded "Humpback Hope," also known in German media as Timmy, is carried by barge into the North Sea, racing against time to save her.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
